page: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores with hyper-threading running at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache and support for AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.

For local storage, the c5d.2xlarge includes a 200 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, offering high-speed local block storage directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The hardware profile lacks dedicated GPU accelerators, making it unsuitable for heavy graphics or large-scale machine learning acceleration.

Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yield average results, as do Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block cached speed of 438.23 GB/sec, while large block uncached reads perform at 51.06 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing benchmarks fall into the weak category. LLM inference benchmarks show average performance on small 135M models but drop to poor or weak tiers for medium 7B models.

This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads requiring fast, temporary local storage. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources and local NVMe storage makes it well-suited for applications such as batch processing, distributed analytics, ad hoc code compilation, and high-performance web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 3.4 GHz on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It includes 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard CPU, memory, and database operations, though it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and larger LLM inference tasks. Lacking a GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that benefit from high-speed local scratch space. It is highly suitable for batch processing, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced compute and local storage performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced memory performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 3.4 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Suitable for batch processing, code compilation, and web serving
